codeReview.summary: The migration to Tamagui in web/components/transactions-table.tsx covers the requested feature goals: heading3 section title styling, body4-sized table text, neutral2 headers, preserved tanstack table wiring/sorting, and animated row entry. I found one non-blocking UI regression in the empty state layout plus one non-blocking convention drift around hardcoded border colors.

issues.description: Empty-state text container uses `<Flex alignItems="center" gap="$spacing8">` without `flexDirection="column"`, so the two empty-state lines render horizontally instead of the previous stacked layout (`flex flex-col`). This is a visual regression from the pre-migration behavior.

Row borders are hardcoded as `rgba(255,255,255,0.12)` instead of token-driven styling (`$surface3` / theme variable). This matches current color output but drifts from the mission's token-only convention and makes future theme changes brittle.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The tamagui-frontend-worker skill still mandates strict TDD (`Write Tests First`) even though this mission's web package does not provide a real frontend test harness for these UI migrations, causing repeated justified deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/tamagui-frontend-worker/SKILL.md:32-33 requires TDD; handoff 2026-04-02T01-19-18-511Z__transactions-table-tamagui__c95de495-5c75-4485-9493-6ddc934faff6.json line 65 documents skipping TDD because web tests are effectively not configured for this.

summary: Reviewed transactions-table-tamagui at commit 258fbc0ed9d6c7237e2fc5fc1b89ea83248f1154 using both the transcript skeleton and commit diff. Core feature requirements are implemented and functional behaviors are preserved, with two non-blocking findings (empty-state layout regression and hardcoded border color usage).
